codeReview.summary: README.md is well-structured and comprehensively covers the project. All required sections per the feature's expectedBehavior are present. Minor inaccuracies exist but are non-blocking.

issues.description: Git clone URL uses 'CliCursorProxyAPI' as repo name, but actual remote is 'opencode-cursor' (https://github.com/ThewindMom/opencode-cursor.git). Users who copy-paste the clone command will get a 404. The cd command on line 64 also uses CliCursorProxyAPI.
Project structure shows 'handler.ts' in src/proxy/ but doesn't list other proxy files present: prompt-builder.ts, tool-loop.ts, formatter.ts, types.ts. The listing is incomplete but not incorrect.
streaming/ section shows 4 files (parser.ts, line-buffer.ts, delta-tracker.ts) but misses ai-sdk-parts.ts which is also present in the directory.

Quickstart includes 'bun run build' step, but 'bun run proxy' works directly from source without a build step. Including build may confuse users into thinking it's required. The build step is only needed for installing the CLI globally.

The /v1/models response example shows only {id, name} per model, but the actual response includes additional fields: object, created, owned_by. This is a minor documentation inaccuracy but doesn't break client compatibility.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The README assumes a 'bun' runtime and doesn't mention npm/node as alternatives despite package.json supporting both. The AGENTS.md or services.yaml doesn't specify whether to document both runtimes or pick one convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Quickstart section only uses 'bun' commands. package.json scripts use 'bun' as primary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The README assumes users know what 'cursor-agent' is and how to install it, but there's no link to installation instructions. The Prerequisites section says 'Install via curl' but doesn't provide the actual command.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Prerequisites says: 'cursor-agent: Install via curl https://cursor.com/install -fsS | bash' but this is a description, not a copy-pasteable command. No link to cursor-agent documentation.

summary: The README is comprehensive and well-organized with all required sections present. The architecture diagram, quickstart, API docs, troubleshooting, and client integration guides are all present and largely accurate. There are no blocking issues — only minor non-blocking inaccuracies: (1) the git clone URL uses a non-existent repo name 'CliCursorProxyAPI' instead of actual 'opencode-cursor', (2) project structure is incomplete but not incorrect, and (3) the build step may be unnecessary for basic proxy usage. The documentation matches the actual implementation for all major functional claims (endpoints, env vars, error codes).
